source_jobs.title: Technical Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
Sigma Prime is looking for a Technical Account Manager to work closely with some of the most technically ambitious teams in Web3.


We’re after someone technical who enjoys building long-term relationships and discussing complex systems.

You’ll sit at the intersection of security, research, and client relationships, working closely with our security and research teams during active engagements, as well as founders and engineers across the ecosystem.

You’ll act as the primary point of contact for inbound enquiries, managing communication from the first conversation through to the end of the engagement.

This is a rare opportunity to work at the centre of complex technical engagements, alongside some of the most experienced security researchers and teams in the ecosystem.



THE COMPANY

Sigma Prime is a blockchain security and distributed systems consultancy focused on securing critical decentralised infrastructure.

Since 2016, we have worked with many of the most prominent teams in the industry, delivering security assessments, protocol research, design consultancy, and specialist engineering services across complex Web3 systems, distributed protocols, zero-knowledge systems, infrastructure, and smart contracts.

We are also the creators and maintainers of Lighthouse, an Ethereum consensus client used by roughly 40% of the network.



THE ROLE

This role is centred around technical trust, communication, and long-term relationships.



YOU WILL:

 - Act as the primary relationship owner for inbound client enquiries

 - Build strong relationships with teams and founders across Ethereum and the broader Web3 ecosystem

 - Lead early technical conversations and help clients navigate the engagement process

 - Coordinate technically accurate proposals and scoping discussions alongside Security Assessment Managers

 - Ensure smooth transitions from proposal into active assessments

 - Maintain visibility across engagement pipelines and ongoing client communication

 - Contribute to technical articles, ecosystem updates, and written content where relevant


YOU MAY FIT THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - Have a strong technical foundation or familiarity with blockchain architecture, security, or distributed systems

 - Enjoy technical discussions and can engage confidently with highly technical teams

 - Take pride in clear, precise written communication

 - Genuinely enjoy relationship-building and becoming a trusted long-term point of contact

 - Have experience in technical coordination, ecosystem-facing roles, developer relations, operations, partnerships, or client engagement

 - Operate well independently in a remote environment


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience writing technical articles, ecosystem updates, newsletters, or long-form technical content




LOCATION

Sigma Prime is headquartered in Sydney, Australia, and operates as a remote-first company with team members globally.

source_jobs.title: Devops Engineer
source_jobs.description:
To improve coverage for a round-the-clock DevOps team, we currently have a strong preference for candidates who can work in the Americas timezone.



Sigma Prime is looking for a self-driven DevOps engineer to help build, automate, operate, and improve infrastructure for Ethereum network development, testing, and production systems.

You will work closely with developers building core decentralised infrastructure, supporting everything from local test environments to high-performance production systems.

This role is centred around reliability, automation, operational ownership, and long-term infrastructure design. We are looking for someone who enjoys improving systems, reducing operational overhead, and building infrastructure that is reproducible and resilient.

This is an opportunity to work alongside experienced Ethereum researchers and engineers on critical infrastructure used across the ecosystem.




THE COMPANY

Sigma Prime is a blockchain security and distributed systems consultancy focused on securing critical decentralised infrastructure.

Since 2016, we have worked with many of the most prominent teams in the industry, delivering security assessments, protocol research, design consultancy, and specialist engineering services across complex Web3 systems, distributed protocols, zero-knowledge systems, infrastructure, and smart contracts.

We are also the creators and maintainers of Lighthouse, an Ethereum consensus client used by roughly 40% of the network.




THE ROLE

You will help build and maintain infrastructure used for Ethereum network development, testing, monitoring, and production systems.

Our work requires both small-scale and large-scale network testing under a variety of real-world conditions. You will create tooling, automation, observability systems, testnets, and deployment infrastructure that help developers validate software behaviour and identify issues early.

Beyond operating infrastructure, we are looking for someone who actively improves it. We value engineers who identify fragile or repetitive processes and turn them into reliable automated systems.




KUBERNETES AND INFRASTRUCTURE


YOU MAY FIT THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - Have strong experience with Kubernetes and Terraform/OpenTofu

 - Have experience administering self-hosted Kubernetes clusters such as RKE2, K3s, or bare-metal Kubernetes

 - Understand Kubernetes architecture, including deployments, ingress, storage, upgrades, rollbacks, and failure recovery

 - Are familiar with Kubernetes networking, including service discovery, DNS, load balancing, and cross-cluster connectivity

 - Have experience operating Kubernetes across multiple infrastructure providers

 - Can optimise infrastructure for cost-efficient, high-throughput blockchain workloads

 - Have experience building CI/CD pipelines for containerised applications

 - Prefer building highly automated infrastructure and deployment systems over manual operational workflows




AUTOMATION AND OPERATIONS


YOU MAY FIT THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - Have strong Linux and server administration experience

 - Are comfortable with shell scripting, systemd, journald, and operational tooling

 - Understand server security, patching, access control, and operational hardening

 - Have strong networking fundamentals, including routing, DNS, and domain management

 - Have experience working with Ansible, inventories, roles, Jinja2 templates, and YAML

 - Enjoy automating repeatable work and improving long-term operational reliability
   
   


BLOCKCHAIN AND ETHEREUM


YOU MAY FIT THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - Understand blockchain concepts such as blocks, forks, and chain state

 - Have familiarity with blockchain networking, peer discovery, and distributed systems behaviour

 - Have a high-level understanding of blockchain signature schemes such as ECDSA

 - Are genuinely enthusiastic about Ethereum and decentralised systems

 - Want to deepen your understanding of Ethereum infrastructure and network operation

source_jobs.title: Business Development & Growth Manager
source_jobs.description:
Sigma Prime is looking for a Business Development & Growth Manager to help expand our security services and develop commercial opportunities around our emerging products.



We’re looking for someone proactive who understands how to build relationships in technical markets, identify opportunities, and turn early conversations into long-term commercial partnerships.

You’ll lead outbound business development for Sigma Prime’s Web3 security assessment services while helping establish the market, partnerships, and customer pipeline for Secstant, our AI-assisted security review tooling currently in development, and Bombora, our Ethereum block builder.

You’ll work directly with Sigma Prime’s directors and collaborate closely with our security, research, engineering, and operations teams.

This is an opportunity to shape the growth function within an established and highly technical Web3 organisation, rather than inheriting an established sales process.




THE COMPANY

Sigma Prime is a blockchain security and distributed systems company focused on securing and building critical decentralised infrastructure.

Since 2016, we have worked with many of the most prominent teams in the industry, delivering security assessments, protocol research, design consultancy, and specialist engineering services across complex Web3 systems, distributed protocols, zero-knowledge systems, infrastructure, and smart contracts.

We are also the creators and maintainers of several production Ethereum products:

 - Lighthouse is a leading Ethereum consensus client and one of the most widely deployed implementations on the network. Written in Rust, Lighthouse has operated in production since the launch of the Beacon Chain and is used by home stakers, professional staking providers, exchanges, and institutional infrastructure operators.

 - Bombora is Sigma Prime’s Ethereum block builder, developed to contribute to builder diversity and strengthen the resilience of Ethereum’s proposer-builder ecosystem.

 - Secstant is our security tooling platform currently in development, designed to improve how complex software is analysed and reviewed.

 - Anchor is an independent Rust implementation of the SSV protocol for Distributed Validator Technology. It introduces client diversity into the SSV ecosystem and helps reduce the operational risks associated with relying on a single implementation.

Our combination of security assessments, research, open-source engineering, and live infrastructure operations gives Sigma Prime a practical understanding of the systems we help secure.




THE ROLE

This role is centred around creating opportunities, building strong ecosystem relationships, and developing a repeatable commercial function across Sigma Prime’s services and products.

You will be responsible for outbound business development for our security assessment services, while also helping us understand and develop the markets for Secstant and Bombora.

As an early member of the growth function, you’ll be encouraged to improve how Sigma Prime tracks opportunities, evaluates channels, and measures commercial performance.

This is not a high-volume transactional sales role. Our work is technical, reputation-driven, and often involves long sales cycles. The role is built on credibility, sound judgment, persistence, and trusted relationships developed over time.




YOU WILL:

 - Identify and approach prospective clients for Sigma Prime’s security assessment, embedded security, and specialist engineering services

 - Build strong relationships with founders, engineers, security leaders, investors, ecosystem organisations, and other decision-makers across Web3

 - Develop commercial and ecosystem relationships for Secstant as the product progresses towards early access and wider availability

 - Identify potential customers, design partners, integrations, and strategic partnerships for Secstant

 - Build relationships across the MEV, searcher, relay, validator, DeFi, and b

notes: Web3 security and research-focused org with a mix of technical and business roles.
